hero.title: Guide to the Development and Promotion of Entrepreneurship
hero.description: In Special Schools: A Comprehensive Guide for Educators, Parents, and Stakeholders.

preface.title: Foreword
paragraphs: The present guide was developed within the framework of the approved European Erasmus+ programme entitled “Our Tradition… Our Entrepreneurship!”.

The programme was designed with the objective of bringing students with disabilities closer to the concept of entrepreneurship by utilizing their abilities and talents through creative activities implemented in school workshops.

The core idea was to combine local tradition with students’ skills. Through ceramics and visual arts workshops carried out in all participating schools, students worked on new techniques, exchanged ideas, and created products inspired by the culture and traditions of each region.
This collaboration brought together students, educators, and specialists from three different countries, creating a valuable experience of knowledge exchange, technical expertise, and good practices.

law.title: Legislation on the Employment of Persons with Disabilities
law.introText: Since 1975, the United Nations has emphasized that persons with disabilities have the right to vocational training, rehabilitation, counselling, and employment placement in order to fully develop their abilities and actively participate in society.
law.unRightsTitle: UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD) - Article 27
unRights: The elimination of discrimination on the basis of disability in all stages of employment.
Protection of rights to fair wages, equal opportunities, and safe and healthy working conditions.
Equal access to labour and trade union rights.
Access to vocational guidance, training, and lifelong learning.
Promotion of employment opportunities, career advancement, and support.
Employment in the public sector and incentives for private sector employment.
Opportunities for work experience in the open labour market.
Vocational rehabilitation and job retention programmes.
law.grLaw: According to the Constitution of Greece, work constitutes a fundamental right (Article 22). Article 21 states that persons with disabilities have the right to benefit from measures ensuring their autonomy and professional integration. Enterprises employing over 50 people are obliged to recruit protected persons at a rate of 8% of their workforce (Law 2643/1998).
law.plLaw: According to the Labor Code, an employer cannot discriminate against an employee based on disability. The main institution supporting employment is the State Fund for the Rehabilitation of Disabled Persons (PFRON), which offers salary subsidies, workplace adaptation reimbursement, and vocational training funding.
law.trLaw: Disability employment is primarily grounded in Article 30 of Labour Law No. 4857. Private-sector employers with 50+ employees must employ at least 3% persons with disabilities, while public-sector employers must employ 4%. Fines for non-compliance are used by İŞKUR to finance entrepreneurship grants, vocational trainings, and sheltered workplaces.

employment.title: Types of Employment
employment.introText: Research findings indicate that labour market integration remains a complex process. Disability does not refer solely to visible physical impairments; it also includes intellectual or psychosocial difficulties, where marginalization is frequently even more pronounced.
employment.puDesc: Employment in the public sector is possible following relevant calls for applications (e.g., ASEP in Greece, EKPSS in Turkey). Certain notices are addressed exclusively to persons with disabilities, while others mandate a specific quota (e.g., 5% of available positions).
employment.prDesc: Private companies, Social Cooperative Enterprises (KoinSEP), and non-profit organizations create employment opportunities or supported employment schemes.
employment.successTitle: Initiative Examples from the Greek Context
employment.frDesc: Persons with disabilities may pursue self-employment, offering greater flexibility and autonomy to adapt professional activities to their abilities.
frBenefits: Tax reduction of €200 automatically applied (67%+ disability).
Exemption or reduction in presumptive taxation (imputed income).
employment.personalAssistantTitle: The Role of the Personal Assistant
employment.personalAssistantDesc: The Personal Assistant (e.g., Law 4837/2021 in Greece) is a modern support mechanism. Their role is not to replace the individual but to provide support for greater autonomy and safety. In the workplace, they assist with organizing daily tasks, mobility, and communication, strengthening job retention and creating positive conditions for both employee and employer.

skills.title: Acquisition of Vocational Skills
skills.introText: Educational and vocational pathways differ across countries, but all aim to equip students with disabilities with the practical and social skills needed for professional integration.

business.title: The Development of Entrepreneurship in Special Schools
business.introText: Entrepreneurship is not simply another school activity. It is a practical way to help students with disabilities take a more active role in society, connecting what they learn at school with the world of work.
business.understandingDesc: In the educational setting, it means students develop creative, innovative, and practical activities that bring them closer to real processes such as producing, managing, and using products or services.
business.importanceDesc: It promotes independence, helps access the labour market, expands social networks, and supports lifelong learning and personal development.
business.formsDesc: This can include student initiatives and small school enterprises (e.g., bazaars), youth entrepreneurship networks (e.g., Junior Achievement), collaborations with social organizations, and educational programs with an entrepreneurial focus.
business.stepsDesc: Effective preparation is a continuous process of discovering strengths and creating meaningful learning experiences.

municipality.title: Family & Municipality Contribution
municipality.introText: The development of entrepreneurship develops within a wider social environment. Both the family and the local municipality play an important role in creating opportunities that support inclusive participation.
municipality.familyDesc: The family is the first and most stable source of support. It serves as a source of psychological support, a supportive network during uncertainty, a practical partner in organizing activities, and a social mediator to expand the individual's network.
municipality.munDesc: Municipalities are close to citizens and can act as drivers of opportunities through institutional support, educational initiatives, and the creation of local entrepreneurial ecosystems.

entrepreneurs.title: Talking with Entrepreneurs
entrepreneurs.introText: As part of strengthening entrepreneurial culture, a series of meetings and interviews with local entrepreneurs was organized. Students engaged in discussions with professionals and observed real working environments.
entrepreneurs.findingsDesc: Discussions showed that employer attitudes are generally positive, though most had no previous experience with employees with disabilities. Employers emphasized the need for state support (workplace adaptations, accessibility). Experiences show that professional inclusion requires cooperation between schools, businesses, and institutional bodies.
entrepreneurs.tableTitle: What Businesses Ask For vs. What It Means in Practice

goodPractices.title: Good Practices in Our Countries
goodPractices.introText: Creating real employment opportunities requires cooperation among schools, businesses, public institutions, and local communities. The following examples highlight ways in which education and businesses can work together to create an inclusive environment.

toolkit.title: Appendices: Teacher Toolkit
toolkit.introText: This toolkit provides structured templates and methods to help educators integrate entrepreneurship and vocational training into special education settings.

footer.conclusionText: Small steps in our schools create equal chances for every student. Through one project, three special schools have come together, reaching many students and opening countless opportunities. The message is clear: every student deserves a chance to learn, grow, and succeed.
